evals.name: neuro-friendly-blogpost
evals.prompt: Turn this paper into a friendly blog post — nice to read, but don't cut any of the actual content.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML blog-post folder: every figure from the paper reproduced unchanged with a colloquialized caption, every reference preserved and given a hover popup with a 'What is it about?' button, sections rewritten in natural conversational language, a translucent table-of-contents sidebar, smooth navigation, and rendered math. No scientific content dropped.
files: Lad et al. - 2026 - Letting the neural code speak Automated characterization of monkey visual neurons through human lan.pdf
assertions: All 7 figures from the paper are reproduced as in the post and every referenced figure file exists on disk (no broken images).
Both tables are rebuilt as real HTML <table> elements rather than screenshots.
Every in-text citation marker (a.cite[data-ref]) resolves to a reference-popup entry that has a non-empty colloquial summary.
Reference popups also include a 'relevance to this paper' note, and each summary is at most ~3 sentences (per spec).
The bibliography is essentially complete: at least 86 reference entries listed (source has ~96).
A translucent table-of-contents sidebar (#toc) is present with at least one link per section.
Equations are kept as real rendered math (MathJax loaded and math delimiters present in the content).
Evidence of section-by-section translation: the post contains 5 or more distinct content <section> blocks (excluding references).
The output is a self-contained openable folder: index.html exists with a figures/ subfolder.

evals.name: neuro-colloquial-register
evals.prompt: Rewrite this paper as a colloquial web version: drop the stiff academic tone, but keep every figure, equation, and reference. Make it pleasant to read and navigate.
evals.expected_output: Same paper rendered as a blog post that clearly shifts the register from formal-academic to natural-conversational while keeping ALL depth, figures, equations, and references. Tests that the skill changes language, not reading level.
files: Lad et al. - 2026 - Letting the neural code speak Automated characterization of monkey visual neurons through human lan.pdf
assertions: All 7 figures from the paper are reproduced as in the post and every referenced figure file exists on disk (no broken images).
Both tables are rebuilt as real HTML <table> elements rather than screenshots.

Reference popups also include a 'relevance to this paper' note, and each summary is at most ~3 sentences (per spec).
The bibliography is essentially complete: at least 86 reference entries listed (source has ~96).
A translucent table-of-contents sidebar (#toc) is present with at least one link per section.
Equations are kept as real rendered math (MathJax loaded and math delimiters present in the content).
Evidence of section-by-section translation: the post contains 5 or more distinct content <section> blocks (excluding references).
The output is a self-contained openable folder: index.html exists with a figures/ subfolder.

evals.name: attention-colorful-blogpost
evals.prompt: Convert this arXiv paper into a colorful, easy-to-read blog post webpage.
evals.expected_output: The Transformer paper as a blog post: the architecture diagram and other figures reproduced exactly, tables rebuilt as clean HTML, every citation preserved with popups, equations rendered, and a pleasant navigable design. Tests generalization to a different field/format.
assertions: All 5 figures (incl. the Transformer architecture diagram) are reproduced as , every referenced figure file exists on disk (no broken images).
The 4 tables are rebuilt as real HTML <table> elements rather than screenshots.
Every in-text citation marker (a.cite[data-ref]) resolves to a reference-popup entry with a non-empty colloquial summary.
Reference popups also include a 'relevance to this paper' note, and each summary is at most ~3 sentences (per spec).
The bibliography is complete: at least 38 reference entries listed (source has 40).
A translucent table-of-contents sidebar (#toc) is present with at least one link per section.
Equations are kept as real rendered math (MathJax loaded and math delimiters present).
Evidence of section-by-section translation: 5 or more distinct content <section> blocks (excluding references).
The output is a self-contained openable folder: index.html exists with a figures/ subfolder.
